channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nairobi Man Pays Tribute to Late Githurai Woman Through Poem
item.description: A young woman, Lorine Okore, tragically passed away in Githurai 44, Kiambu County, leaving family, friends, and the local community heartbroken. Her body was discovered in a trench, and while some rep...

item.title: A Romance and Business Partnership Built on Understanding Beyond Words
item.description: In 2013, Federica, a pastry chef from Milan, moved to a small town in Veneto to work with a Michelin-starred chef. It was there she met Oskar, an Australian chef, whose presence initially seemed tempo...

item.title: Sundowns aim for back-to-back wins against Polokwane
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns will face Polokwane City for the second time in just a few days, looking to build on their midweek 1-0 victory. The win keeps their title hopes alive as they remain at the top of the...

item.title: NYC Police Release Body-Cam Footage of Machete-Wielding Suspect's Fatal Shooting at Grand Central
item.description: New York City police have released body-camera footage of officers fatally shooting a man who had attacked three people with a machete at the 42nd Street-Grand Central subway station. The man, identif...

item.title: Investigating Martin Romualdez's Ties to Tacloban Golf Club and Property Deals
item.description: Former House Speaker Martin Romualdez is under investigation for potential money laundering, with the Office of the Ombudsman looking into several properties linked to him and his associates. The inve...

item.title: Japan Brings Major Northern Wildfire Under Control After 11 Days
item.description: Northern Japan witnessed one of the largest wildfires in decades, which authorities have now reported as under control after a relentless 11-day effort. The blaze, which erupted in late April, affecte...

item.title: Weekend Sports: Premier League, F1, and World Snooker Final Coverage
item.description: This weekend promises a thrilling mix of sports coverage, starting with the Premier League as crucial games unfold, including matchups that could impact the title race and relegation. Arsenal faces Fu...

item.title: Bryant Kipkorir Kemboi Graduates with Master’s in Civil Engineering from Michigan Technological University
item.description: Bryant Kipkorir Kemboi, a young man from Nandi, Kenya, celebrated a major academic milestone after earning his Master’s degree in Civil Engineering from Michigan Technological University in the United...

item.title: Cosatu holds main Workers’ Day event in Polokwane with Deputy President speaking
item.description: On 1 May 2026, Cosatu hosted its main Workers’ Day event at the Old Peter Mokaba Stadium in Polokwane, Limpopo. The highlight of the rally was a keynote address delivered by Deputy President Paul Mash...

item.title: Western Media’s Institutional Inertia in a Changing Multipolar World
item.description: The article argues that much of Western legacy media and cultural production is stuck in what sociologist William F. Ogburn termed “institutional inertia,” meaning outdated mental models are being app...

item.title: Sinaloa Governor and Mayor Temporarily Step Down After US Drug Trafficking Indictment
item.description: Two prominent figures from Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum’s party in Sinaloa state have temporarily stepped down after being indicted by US authorities for drug trafficking. The indictment accuse...

item.title: The History and Culture of Saint Pierre and Miquelon, France’s North American Territory
item.description: Saint Pierre and Miquelon is a small French territory off the coast of Newfoundland, Canada, home to about 6,000 people. Unlike Newfoundland, which joined Canada in 1948, these islands remained French...

item.title: Remembering Errol Wendel Chen: Young Advocate Killed in Negros Occidental
item.description: Errol Wendel Chen, a 24-year-old advocate for farmers in the Philippines, was among 19 people killed in an operation in Negros Occidental on April 19, 2026. Official accounts labeled some victims as s...

item.title: Sebastian Sawe's Parents Celebrate His World Record and Promise to Buy Them Car and House
item.description: Sebastian Sawe, the 31-year-old Kenyan marathoner, recently made history by setting a new world record at the London Marathon, completing the race in 1:59:30. His parents, Simion and Emily, who watche...

item.title: Florey: Affordable Living with Great Schools and Local Amenities
item.description: Florey, a suburb in Canberra, offers a compelling mix of convenience, community, and affordability, making it a strong contender for homebuyers. Located near Lake Ginninderra and just minutes from Bel...

item.title: Barbierro: A Safe and Creative Space for the LGBTQ+ Community in the Philippines
item.description: Barbierro Barbershop is one of the Philippines’ first queer-friendly barbershops, providing a space where haircuts and grooming services are based on hair type, length, and client preference rather th...

item.title: Kitengela Family Seeks Husband and Son Missing in South Africa for Over a Decade
item.description: A family from Kitengela, Kajiado County, has been living with uncertainty and pain for over 10 years since their son, Silas Kipkorir, went missing in South Africa. Kipkorir, a hardworking and loving h...

item.title: Musely raises $360M in non-dilutive funding to support growth
item.description: Musely, a telemedicine platform offering compounded treatments for skin, hair, and menopause care, has secured over $360 million from General Catalyst’s Customer Value Fund (CVF), which specializes in...

item.title: West Bengal 2026 Election: Falta Votes to Be Counted Tomorrow for 293 Seats
item.description: In the 2026 West Bengal Assembly elections, a delay has occurred in counting the votes for the Falta constituency. While the results for most constituencies are set to be declared, the counting of vot...

item.title: Fatal Vehicle Crash at Portland Health Club Involves Explosive Device
item.description: Early Saturday morning, a tragic incident occurred at the Multnomah Athletic Club in Portland, Oregon, when a vehicle crashed into the facility around 3 a.m. Authorities confirmed that one person died...
